This is a legitimate TDD outcome. When the simplest implementation (`return []`) already satisfies the new test, the test passes immediately. The two previous tests also expect `[]` for their inputs, so the hardcoded `return []` covers all three cases. The implementation will need to change when a test arrives that expects a non-empty result (like the block pattern survival test or the blinker evolution test).
